title: issue-type-and-slug-fields: add type + slug to the record
body:
# Goal

Add two first-class structural fields to every issue
record:

- **`type`**: one of `bug`, `feature`, `epic`, `research`,
  `roadmap`, `unspecified`. The work-shape marker.
- **`slug`**: an optional kebab-case orientation handle
  (e.g. `agent-ready`, `cli-skeleton`, `sync-push-pull`).
  Unique across open issues when set. The human-friendly
  alternative to the 7-hex id.

End state: every issue has both fields available; `jjf
new --type bug --slug fix-foo ...` sets them; `jjf ls
--type feature` filters by type; `jjf show <slug-or-id>`
resolves either; `jjf ready` (forthcoming) sorts by type
priority (bug > feature > research > epic > unspecified,
with roadmap excluded — it's not work to do).

# Why now

`jjf ready` (`69d5e1b`) is sketched against `Bug` types
(blocked by `199ed91` Bug → Issue rename). Both tickets
need the type field to do their jobs correctly. And the
slug field, which has been useful as a title-prefix
convention throughout this session (`cli-skeleton:`,
`agent-ready:`, etc.), is structurally similar metadata
worth promoting alongside type: same spec bump, same
schema-version cost, same general-purpose value.

Both fields rejected as labels-by-convention for the
same reason: discipline erodes. Type and slug are
structural, queryable, and want first-class treatment:

- **Type** is structural everywhere else: GitHub (added
  2024), Gitea, Jira, Beads. `ready`'s priority logic
  depends on type being meaningful and stable.
- **Slug** has saved hours of grep-by-title operations
  already; agents naturally reach for stable handles.
  `jjf show agent-ready` beats `jjf ls | grep agent` for
  every consumer.

Both ride the `199ed91` (`bug_id` → `issue_id` rename)
v1 → v2 spec bump. One bump, two new fields.

# Sequencing

This ticket sits in a three-ticket chain:

1. `199ed91` (Bug → Issue rename, v1 → v2 spec bump). Lands
   first because it's the wider rename pass; this ticket
   adds two fields on top of the renamed types.
2. **This ticket** (`type` + `slug` fields). Lands second,
   against the renamed types. Fold into the same v2 spec
   bump if `199ed91` hasn't shipped yet; otherwise rev
   to v2.1.
3. `69d5e1b` (`agent-ready`). Lands third, against the
   typed schema, so its priority sort has a real field
   to read.

# Scope

## Storage

### `type` field

- Add `IssueType` enum to `crates/jjf-storage/`:
  ```rust
  pub enum IssueType {
      Bug,
      Feature,
      Epic,
      Research,
      Roadmap,
      Unspecified,
  }
  ```
  Serde rename to lowercase; default = `Unspecified`.
- Add `type: IssueType` field to `IssueRecord`. Place
  it after `status`, before `labels`.
- Add `Op::SetType { issue_id, kind: IssueType }`.
- Add `IssueDraft.type_: Option<IssueType>` (default
  None → `Unspecified`).
- Extend `Storage::update(UpdateFields { type_:
  Option<Option<IssueType>>, ... })`. Double-Option
  follows the assignee pattern.
- `Storage::create_issue` emits `Op::SetType` in the
  multi-op create commit when type is non-default.

### `slug` field

- Add `slug: Option<String>` field to `IssueRecord`.
  Place it after `title`, before `body`.
- **Validation:** kebab-case, `[a-z0-9-]+`, no leading
  or trailing hyphen, no consecutive hyphens. Length
  3–48 chars. Reject anything else at the storage layer
  with a typed error (`Error::InvalidSlug { slug:
  String, reason: SlugReason }`).
- **Uniqueness:** unique across OPEN issues (status =
  open). Closed issues' slugs are released — a slug can
  be reused after the original closes. Enforced at
  write time: `create_issue` and `update` validate
  before commit by walking `list_ids` + reading each
  open issue's slug.
- Add `Op::SetSlug { issue_id, slug: Option<String> }`.
  None = clear the slug.
- Add `IssueDraft.slug: Option<String>` (default None).
- Extend `UpdateFields { slug: Option<Option<String>>,
  ... }`. None = leave alone; Some(Some(x)) = set;
  Some(None) = clear.

### Resolver

- New `Storage::resolve(handle: &str) -> Result<IssueId>`
  that takes either a 7-hex id OR a slug:
  - If `handle` parses as `IssueId`, return as-is.
  - Else walk `list_ids` + read; return the id whose
    record's `slug` matches. Error if no match
    (`Error::SlugNotFound`).
  - Performance: read-all is fine for v1, same as `ls`.
    Index optimization is a follow-up.
- Every CLI verb that takes an id should accept a slug
  via `Storage::resolve` first. Mutating verbs that
  flow into `Storage::update` etc. resolve once at the
  CLI boundary and pass the id down.

## Spec

- `docs/storage-format.md`:
  - §3 (`IssueRecord`): add `type` field (enum,
    default `unspecified`, position after `status`) and
    `slug` field (`Option<String>`, default `null`,
    position after `title`).
  - §3.x (new subsection): slug validation rules and
    uniqueness scope (open-only).
  - §5.2 (`Jjf-Op` vocabulary): add `set-type` op
    (`{ "issue_id": "...", "type": "..." }`) and
    `set-slug` op (`{ "issue_id": "...", "slug":
    "..." | null }`).
  - §5.7 (multi-op create): list `set-type` and
    `set-slug` among the ops the create commit may
    emit.
- This is a v2 spec change. If `199ed91` ships first
  as v2, fold into that. Otherwise rev to v2.1.

## CLI

### `--type` flag (per `7100b51` original scope)

- `jjf new --type <bug|feature|epic|research|roadmap>`:
  optional. Default `unspecified`. Clap `value_enum`.
- `jjf update <id> --type <kind>`: optional.
- `jjf ls --type <kind>`: optional filter. Repeatable
  with OR semantics (`--type bug --type feature` →
  matches either).
- `jjf show` surfaces type in plain-text and `--json`.

### `--slug` flag

- `jjf new --slug <kebab-case>`: optional.
- `jjf update <id> --slug <kebab-case>`: optional set.
- `jjf update <id> --unset-slug`: clear the slug.
  `--slug` and `--unset-slug` are mutually exclusive
  (clap `conflicts_with`).
- `jjf show <slug-or-id>`: accept slug as the
  positional. Resolve via `Storage::resolve`.
- `jjf ls --slug <pattern>`: optional filter. Substring
  match against the slug field (so `--slug agent`
  matches both `agent-ready` and `agent-ergonomics`).
- All other id-taking verbs (`update`, `comment`,
  `close`, `open`, `label add|rm`) accept slug
  positional too.

### Plain-text show format

Include both new lines:

```
<id>  [<status>]  type: <kind>
<title>
slug: <slug-or-(none)>
labels: <comma-sep or (none)>
...
```

### Error kinds

New in `docs/cli-json.md`:

- `invalid_slug` (exit 2) with `details.reason:
  "bad_charset" | "too_short" | "too_long" |
  "leading_hyphen" | "trailing_hyphen" |
  "consecutive_hyphens"`.
- `slug_collision` (exit 2) with `details.slug` and
  `details.conflicts_with: <id>` (the open issue
  already holding that slug).
- `slug_not_found` (exit 2) with `details.handle:
  "<the-string-the-user-typed>"`.

## Docs

- `docs/cli-json.md`: update every verb's example
  envelope. New flags documented; new error kinds
  added.
- `README.md`: "What jjforge tracks" section mentions
  type and slug. Quick-tour shows `jjf new --type
  feature --slug agent-ready -t "..."` and `jjf show
  agent-ready`.
- `CLAUDE.md`: "Creating a new issue" recommends
  `--type` and `--slug` on every `jjf new`. Queries
  section gets `jjf show <slug>` and `jjf ls --type
  bug` examples.

## Migration

The 9 existing issues filed pre-this-ticket have no
`type` and no `slug`. Read-path defaults: type =
`Unspecified`, slug = `None`. Backfill in a one-time
pass:

```bash
# Set type on all existing issues
jjf update 9566f52 --type roadmap --slug roadmap
jjf update <each-epic> --type epic --slug <epic-slug>
jjf update <each-feature-ticket> --type feature --slug <slug-from-title>
jjf update <each-bug-ticket> --type bug --slug <slug-from-title>
```

Slugs to assign (titles already follow the convention,
so extract from titles):

- `9566f52` (roadmap) → `--slug roadmap`
- `a5f8122` (epic:mvp-cli) → `--type epic --slug mvp-cli`
- `54caedd` (epic:mvp-sync) → `--type epic --slug mvp-sync`
- `f21b950` (epic:mvp-storage) → `--type epic --slug mvp-storage`
- `5a755ec` (epic:agent-ergonomics) → `--type epic --slug agent-ergonomics`
- `99f01ed` (epic:multi-client) → `--type epic --slug multi-client`
- `585b84b` (epic:project-agent-orchestration) → `--type epic --slug project-agent-orchestration`
- `199ed91` (Bug → Issue rename) → `--type bug --slug rename-bug-to-issue`
- `69d5e1b` (agent-ready) → `--type feature --slug agent-ready`
- `7100b51` (this ticket) → `--type feature --slug issue-type-and-slug-fields`

Document the recipe in the closing comment. Not
strictly necessary (defaults are valid) but `ready`
and slug-resolution both work better with them
populated.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `IssueType` enum + serde roundtrip test.
- [ ] `slug` field + validation tests covering each
  rejection reason.
- [ ] `Storage::resolve(handle)` accepts id OR slug;
  tests cover both paths and the not-found error.
- [ ] Slug uniqueness enforced at write time;
  integration test: create with slug X, try create
  with slug X again → `slug_collision`.
- [ ] Slug uniqueness scope is OPEN only: create with
  slug X, close, create again with slug X → succeeds.
- [ ] `Op::SetType` and `Op::SetSlug` parse + replay +
  history-reader surface.
- [ ] `Storage::update(... type_: ..., slug: ...)`
  lands appropriate trailers; verified via
  `read_history`.
- [ ] `jjf new --type bug --slug fix-foo -t "x" -F -`
  creates with both fields set; `jjf show fix-foo`
  resolves it.
- [ ] `jjf ls --type bug` filters work.
- [ ] `jjf ls --slug agent` substring-matches.
- [ ] `jjf update <id> --slug new-handle` mutation works.
- [ ] `jjf update <id> --unset-slug` clears.
- [ ] All id-taking verbs accept slug.
- [ ] `docs/storage-format.md`, `docs/cli-json.md`,
  `README.md`, `CLAUDE.md` updated.
- [ ] Migration recipe documented in closing comment;
  optionally apply it as part of the same round (the
  10 existing issues are small enough to backfill
  inline).
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` stays green
  (currently 185).

# Out of scope

- `jjf ready`'s type-priority sort: that's `69d5e1b`'s
  job, depends on this.
- Renaming `Bug` → `Issue`: that's `199ed91`. If this
  ticket files first, use `Bug` types; the rename pass
  sweeps them.
- A persistent slug index. Read-all is fine until
  proven slow.
- Slug history (which past slugs an issue held).
  Trailers carry it implicitly; surfacing in `jjf show`
  is a follow-up.
- Reserved-slug list (preventing `--slug all` /
  `--slug help` etc. that might collide with future
  CLI verbs). v1 trusts the operator; if collisions
  surface, add a deny-list later.
- Mandatory typing or slugging on create. Defaults
  are valid; the operator chooses when to care.
- Subtypes (`bug:regression` vs `bug:new`,
  `feature:enhancement` vs `feature:new`). Labels can
  carry that nuance.

# Pointers

- Enum precedent: `Status` enum in
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/record.rs`.
- Op precedent: `Op::SetStatus`, `Op::SetTitle` in
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/op.rs`.
- Multi-op create pattern: `Storage::create_issue`
  conditionally emits ops based on populated fields.
- `UpdateFields` double-Option pattern: existing
  precedent for "leave alone vs set vs unset" (see
  `assignee`).
- Validation pattern: `BugId::parse` in
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/id.rs` is the structural
  precedent — typed error, charset check, length
  check. `IssueType::from_str` and slug validation
  follow the same shape.
- `Storage::list_ids` is the enumeration primitive
  for the uniqueness check.
- Spec §3.1, §5.2, §5.7 all get edits.
- Dependent: `69d5e1b` (`agent-ready`).
